entities.name: AI
entities.context: Technology that has reduced the marginal cost of producing fake/inflated job applications to zero

entities.name: Hiring Meritocracy
entities.context: The belief that hiring rewards honest, qualified applicants — V argues job seekers have rationally abandoned this

entities.name: Marginal Cost of BS
entities.context: V's framing: AI has driven the cost of producing fraudulent applications to effectively zero

entities.name: ATS (Applicant Tracking Systems)
entities.context: Systems that reward keyword optimization over substance, contributing to hiring market dysfunction

entities.name: Gartner
entities.context: Research firm that projects 1 in 4 job applicants will be fake by 2028

entities.name: Hiring Market Trust Collapse
entities.context: V's thesis that the entire hiring ecosystem faces a trust breakdown — employers can't find real talent, candidates can't get noticed honestly

entities.name: Keyword Optimization
entities.context: Gaming strategy where applicants stuff resumes with ATS-friendly keywords rather than demonstrating real competence

entities.name: Rational Actor Theory (in hiring)
entities.context: V's argument that when there's no penalty for lying, rational actors will optimize for gaming the system over honest applications

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company operating in the hiring domain — this position directly supports its value proposition

relationships.context: AI is the causal force that reduced the marginal cost of fake applications to zero
Zero-cost fraud is the mechanism driving trust collapse in hiring
ATS systems incentivize keyword optimization over substance, enabling gaming
Rational actors abandon meritocracy when gaming offers better odds
Gartner's projection of 1-in-4 fake applicants by 2028 quantifies the trust collapse
V's company is positioned to address this trust breakdown in hiring
AI is the root technological driver breaking the hiring ecosystem
Keyword gaming undermines meritocratic hiring by rewarding optimization over competence
